title: Trading bots the future of trading or just a flashy scam
author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
selftext: I have been seeing bots everywhere promising 10 percent daily returns from Genesis to Binance auto traders Has anyone actually profited consistently or is it just automated hype?

comments.author: KD_Hub
comments.body:
Hype around "fully automated" wealth is the biggest red flag in the industry right now. 

I’ve seen so many people lose their shirts because they trusted a bot that didn't know how to react when a major exchange went down or a tweet sent prices spiraling. 

The real future isn't about replacing the trader with a machine, but using AI to filter out the noise so the human can make the final, smartest decision based on actual context.

replies.author: Emergency_Style4515
replies.body:
Sudden market movements are actually when an automated system should have the advantage of being able to act quickly. 

A poorly designed car getting into an accident doesn’t undermine the idea of cars.

replies.author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
replies.body: Sudden moves are actually where automation should shine because it can react instantly. A bad bot failing in chaos doesn’t mean automation is flawed any more than a bad car crash means cars don’t work. The issue is poor design and overpromising, not automation itself.

replies.author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
replies.body: 100% agree. “Fully automated” wealth is the biggest red flag right now. Markets don’t break because of charts, they break because of events. Bots don’t handle exchange outages or sudden news well without human oversight. The real edge is AI filtering the noise so a human can make the final call with actual context.

comments.author: ApocalypseParadise
comments.body:
Not a scam in general, but there are scammers making ridiculous claims. RenTec and other top funds have been using AI for 2-3 decades, and algotrading bots for another 1-2 decades, and beating everyone else as a result -- 66% ROI/yr with RenTec medallion fund, for example. Almost all hedge funds are using AI /ML now, though with human traders in the loop whenever possible. But a lot of “AI trading bot” marketing out there promises unrealistic returns, like 10%+ daily percentage gains, which is marketing hype. 

The AI bots that actually work tend to be custom rule‑based systems people design for specific markets or setups that pro human traders use successfully; but the AI can check far more things, far quicker, and far more precisely. Then simple ML can optimize all the parameters, automatically, and adjust for new regimes.

replies.author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
replies.body: Yeah, agreed. AI itself isn’t a scam, the scammy part is the marketing. Top funds like RenTech have used AI and quant systems for decades with humans in the loop. What’s unrealistic are the “10% daily” bot claims. The stuff that actually works is usually custom, rule based systems built on real trading edges, with ML just optimizing and adapting, not some magic money printer.

replies.author: ApocalypseParadise
replies.body: Exactly. The experts automate & use AI/ML as much as possible, then they still have pro traders make the final call & who'll have knowledge of things the AI can't know, unless it's HFT or sub-minute trading.

comments.author: Adventurous_Chart360
comments.body: check out [https://tradebotengine.com/](https://tradebotengine.com/) . Look at historic data and been performing consistently for swing trades.

replies.author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
replies.body: thanks dude. Will check on that

comments.author: Party_Musician_9914
comments.body:
Not a scam at all! 
Although there will always be scammers.

replies.author: Adventurous_Bee_7244
replies.body: Definitely not a scam, just a space full of bad actors and unrealistic promises.

comments.author: StevenVinyl
comments.body: I mean... they just post returns on some strategies (maybe generated by AI?), aka hype posting and pure marketing. That's what works today.
